name: ExtensionAppointment_PreviousAppointment
title: R5: The previous appointment in a series (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Appointment.previousAppointment` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Appointment.previousAppointment` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Appointment)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Appointment.previousAppointment` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Appointment)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Appointment.previousAppointment` has a context of Appointment based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Appointment`.
Element `Appointment.previousAppointment` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: The previous appointment in a series (new)
element.comment:
Element `Appointment.previousAppointment` has a context of Appointment based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Appointment`.
Element `Appointment.previousAppointment` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
This property is intended for use when representing a series of related appointments.  For example, in a nuclear medicine procedure, where there is an appointment for the injection of the isotopes, and then a subsequent appointment for the scan, the scan appointment would refer to the injection appointment via Appointment.previousAppointment.  For representing recurring appointments, see the guidance on recurring vs. series appointments.

element.short: The previous appointment in a series
element.definition: The previous appointment in a series of related appointments.
element.comment: This property is intended for use when representing a series of related appointments.  For example, in a nuclear medicine procedure, where there is an appointment for the injection of the isotopes, and then a subsequent appointment for the scan, the scan appointment would refer to the injection appointment via Appointment.previousAppointment.  For representing recurring appointments, see the guidance on recurring vs. series appointments.
